reviews.review_description: I've had beatsaber for 1 year and it's really fun however you shouldn't buy the game if you won't mod it cause the base game is very boring with mid songs and for other albums you have to pay 2 dollars but if you mod your game you get hella songs like 11,000 for free the process is kinda complicated so I recommend just watching a YouTube tutorial

WARNING: LONG REVIEW. I don’t know about you but I always tend to read reviews before I buy whatever app that I intend to get. I saw a lot of mixed reviews about the game (mainly 5 stars but there were a lot of 1 stars) like “the game is a total rip off because the 30$ price tag isn’t worth it for only 10 free packs” and “it’s really hard to get better at this game because all of the songs are evenly balanced” or “the game sucks because i can’t get more than 15 seconds in an expert in the new packs”. 1st review solution: think about it differently, 2nd review solution: play easier songs, 3rd review solution: just get better i don’t know about you but 10 free packs is a lot of free songs (I counted when I was playing on my friends account and there is I think 65-70 free songs on it). It’s also a really good exercise source if you do harder modes like expert or expert+ i always tend to burn at least 500 calories each time I play. I love linkin park so when I saw that they had a bundle for 2 linkin park packs (deal only on mobile app) and the game bought it. It’s a good price because it’s the game + 20 add on songs for only 50$ when if you bought the game and then both packs separately you pay about 75$ so you get a easy 15$ off
I personally really hope that you get this because it’s an amazing game

reviews.review_title: I will love this game forever.
reviews.review_description: Every hater is just a 5yr old lying because i've been plaging for like 3-4 years and once i tried learning to get better, it was a long process but it was easy it i had a lot of fun learning and made a lot of friends. This game is just perfect down to the last detail especially stand alone nodded it's just pure good times and funny moments of my life i love it tbh.

reviews.review_title: Changed review after 6 months of playing
reviews.review_description: I thought I was sick of the songs after a week but then realized that every new level (hard, expert, etc) is like playing a new song. Also, the way to get better is muscle memory so you need to play them a lot. I'm not sick of the songs at all. I really like the Monstercat music packs. I have advanced from hard (where I started) to now being able to play some expert+ songs after 6 months of playing. I'll be playing for yrs. Plz keep adding new EDM packs!

I've been playing this game for multiplayer exercise since its launch, and it was great for years. I own all music packs as they came out.
UNFORTUNATELY,  Meta ducked it up 2 months ago. Now the only other people whom are playing are new, and don't own ANY music packs. That forces all the Beat Saber veterans whom paid hundreds of dollars for all the packs can't even play the songs we bought. In multiplayer there is a tick box, comes ticked by default, says you "own all songs". That will put you in a full music lobby, but the new people don't have the songs so the game will then default to a song among the selected, to play that everyone has. That being a default, a Not made to dance original Beat saber song, not a paid one. Ever. Seriously,  Meta screwed over ALL the people whom paid. I deleted Beat saber today. I feel robbed.

Now y’all are telling me we’re paying $30-£22 for a hitting beat blocks but we can’t get popular songs for free (some) I get that it’s affordable but some should be free? Or there should be a monthly subscription where u can play all the songs for a reasonable price but still if we’re paying that much we need some nice and free songs that we can play not some beats.. overall it’s a good game and I hope u can understand!

I thought I was getting good at the game. I had beaten some of the hardest levels on expert. I decided to try expert+. I tried the first levels on it and the first three ost packs were HORRIBLY designed. HALF OF THE BEATS WERE LITERALLY IMPOSSIBLE TO REACH, MOST OF THE BEATS DONT EVEN GO ALONG WITH THE MUSIC, AND ARE JUST THERE TO MAKE THE SONG HARDER, THERE IS TOO MANY BEATS THAT BLOCK MY VISION, MAKING ME NOT ABLE TO SEE THE BEATS BEHIND THOSE BEATS, and I could go on and on. I also know it’s not a skill issue, because I have beaten many extremely hard levels, like “circle hit”, and “WHAT THE CAT”, ECT.

Although I have had this game for a good while, I just noticed that it has some mixed reviews, and as a Beat Saber veteran, I’ve come to clear the air. To all of the people saying there aren’t enough free song packs; challenge yourself in different ways! I, for one, play a lot of online rounds. Crank up the difficulty on songs, and they’ll feel entirely different. I also saw some reviews saying that the songs were all just plain hard/impossible. Play on an easier game mode, or you can manually adjust the speed of any given song by clicking on “practice”. 
The way I see it, there really is no valid reason to hate on this game. Any complaints that I see in the bad reviews seem like they could easily be worked around. 
Now for my actual review; as I said above, I’ve been playing this game for a good while. I typically only play in online mode by now, I find the competition fun. Once the difficulty is high enough, it really does become a good workout game. One thing to note before you buy the game is that people can find the game either incredibly easy or incredibly hard. I enjoy putting family members and friends into it to see how they do, and I’ve gotten very mixed responses. Some find easy mode incredibly… well… easy, and immediately switch to hard mode. Others, will scream and try to dodge all of the note blocks. I know this game is pretty popular within the VR community, and while videos online can make it look easy, I would reccomend really thinking about how YOU would react to blocks flying at your head. All said and done, I LOVE this game. It never stops being fun, and I highly reccomend for any rhythm game fans!

reviews.review_title: One of the best games ever.

I understand they can’t just let people make all the levels because of copie writing songs and the fact the wouldn’t make any money off them. But if they just took a level from the modding community with a song that can be used for free every day or week and put it in vanilla it would give people a reason to play more.










And custom sabers

Fantastic game.

reviews.review_description: I love this game and it gets me moving. It makes me sweat and helps me lose weight. I thank my hubby for introducing me to this game and it's fun. Please add AC/DC pack, selena gomez pack, nickelback pack, three days grace pack, country pack, 90s pack, 80s pack, 70s pack ect that would be great. I hope beat sabor sees this.

I really enjoy playing this game, I've had it over a year and still have not played every song. It's all very challenging and a great workout. Some song I love so much that I have no problem playing them all the time. The weekend pack is my favorite and various songs from each pack. I have not modded the game at all and it's still very enjoyable. You can play with people but I just play alone. The songs are more than enough to keep busy, the songs are of many different genres. I play almost everything one level above hard....some stuff is so fast that hard is the highest level I can do comfortably. Each song is just different when it comes to the ease of hitting notes. It's my favorite game on vr hand down and can't get off with at least playing one song. The song maps are fun to look at, if you like the demo version, then you will love the full version. Once you get bored, then you can trouble yourself with a mod, but it will be a while before that. If you want to disguise your workouts as something enjoyable, get it! Just make a playlist and do 10 songs. It will fly by! Then pair it with a boxing game or something else that gets you moving. You will melt that fat off!

I am a 45-year-old and in need of both knees and a hip replacement, perimenopausal, and in constant pain. 
I absolutely love this game!!!
It allows me to get moving when I really don't want to and have fun doing so. I can change intensity and speed to match my daily mobility issues and pain levels
You can switch between sitting down and standing up mid game, or between games without changing settings. 
Generally a lot of fun and definitely one that gets played daily by this old broken bird!!

reviews.review_title: Make sure you know what music u like
reviews.review_description: Great game, it takes time to get really good but don’t let that discourage you from buying. If you buy an album make sure you know what you’re buying meaning you like the music and songs. If you’re not sure try listening to the songs on your phone or something. Again great game.

I’ve been playing Beat Saber for a long time, but getting into mods was honestly a whole journey for me. It took me about 3–6 months of trial and error, watching tutorials, messing things up, reinstalling, and trying again before I finally understood how everything worked. At first, it felt confusing and overwhelming, especially dealing with files, mod loaders, and game versions. There were moments where I almost gave up, but I kept pushing through because I really loved the game.

Now, I can confidently say I’m an advanced modder. Once everything finally clicked, it completely changed my experience. Mods opened up so many possibilities — custom songs, insane visual effects, advanced mapping tools, and so much more. Learning how everything connects behind the scenes made me appreciate the game even more.

My absolute favorite things are Noodle Extensions and Chroma levels. Noodle Extensions makes maps feel alive with crazy movement, custom note paths, and unique mechanics that go way beyond the base game. Chroma adds insane lighting and color effects that turn levels into full-on light shows. When both are combined, it doesn’t even feel like the same game anymore — it feels like a VR concert experience.

Overall, modding Beat Saber was 100% worth the struggle. It took patience, but now I get to enjoy some of the most creative and mind-blowing custom levels out there. If you’re thinking about getting into mods, don’t give up — once you figure it out, it changes everything.

reviews.review_title: Not suitable for people with mobility pr
reviews.review_description: When I first tried this game, I liked it. It seemed like it was going to be a really good game, but there are no beginner level slower songs that are able to be used by people with mobility issues. I find that all the songs are too fast and adjusting the settings doesn’t help at all.

developer_response.body: Thank you for sharing this with us. A couple of things that might help: in Modifiers, you can turn off walls and bombs to make things a bit more manageable. And in Practice Mode, you can slow down the speed of any level to a pace that works for you.
